name: server
context: Accept x402 stablecoin payments as an HTTP server using Express, Hono, or Next.js middleware
info: This skill adds x402 payment acceptance to any HTTP server. The server-side integration is a single middleware that intercepts configured routes, returns 402 with payment requirements when no payment is present, and verifies/settles payments through a facilitator when a PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header arrives. Supported frameworks include Express, Hono, and Next.js. The middleware needs a wallet address to receive payments, a facilitator URL, and a route-to-price mapping. Settlement happens automatically — the facilitator handles onchain submission.
triggers: accept x402 payments on my server
add x402 to my express app
add a payment wall to my endpoint

sections.title: Express middleware
sections.body: Install `@x402/express`. Import `paymentMiddleware`. Call `app.use(paymentMiddleware(routeMap, options))` before your route handlers. The middleware auto-handles 402 responses, verification, and settlement. See [[ethereum/x402#sdks]] for all available SDK packages.

sections.title: Hono middleware
sections.body: Install `@x402/hono`. Import `paymentMiddleware`. Apply to specific paths with `app.use("/path/*", paymentMiddleware(routeMap, options))`. Same configuration shape as Express.

sections.title: Route configuration
sections.body: Each entry maps `"METHOD /path"` to a payment requirement object with `price` (USD string), `network` (chain name), and optional `config.description`. Multiple routes can have different prices and chains.

sections.title: Settlement flow
sections.body: After verification, the middleware calls the facilitator's `/settle` endpoint. The facilitator submits the signed payment transaction onchain and returns a receipt (see [[ethereum/x402#facilitator]]). The server includes this receipt in the `PAYMENT-RESPONSE` header. Settlement is asynchronous — the server returns the resource immediately after verification, and settlement completes in the background. The [[ethereum/x402/client]] can inspect the `PAYMENT-RESPONSE` header for the transaction hash.
